docs.title: PlayerData API
docs.text:
GraphQL API for accessing athlete, session, performance, and raw GPS data captured on the PlayerData platform.

Designed for technology partners, clubs, federations, analytics teams, research integrations.

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• API Overview — endpoints, schema, sample queries
  • Authentication — OAuth2 grant types
  • Concepts — Organisation, Club, Session, Athlete
  • Metrics — distance, speed, intensity, raw GPS
  • Python SDK — pip install playerdatapy
  • R wrapper — remotes::install_github("PlayerData/playerdatar")
  • Other Clients — curl, JS, Go, anything that speaks HTTP

docs.title: At a glance
docs.text:
query SessionDetails($id: ID!) {
  trainingSession(id: $id) {
    startTime
    sessionParticipations {
      athlete { name }
      athleteMetricSet {
        totalDistanceM
        maxSpeedKph
      }
    }
  }
}

GraphQL endpoint: https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphql. Authenticate via OAuth2 — see Authentication.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• Repo: PlayerData/playerdatapy (official Python SDK)
  • GraphiQL Playground: app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphiql — live schema, autocomplete, interactive queries. Runs against production; mutations affect live data.
  • Support: support@playerdata.com

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: PlayerData API uses OAuth 2.0. Two grant types depending on integration.

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: Purpose URL GraphQL API https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphql OAuth Authorisation https://app.playerdata.co.uk/oauth/authorize OAuth Token/Refresh https://app.playerdata.co.uk/oauth/token Sign Out https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/auth/identities/sign_out

docs.title: Grant types
docs.text:
Authorisation Code Grant — user-based access.

  • Interactive sign-in
  • Access mirrors what user sees in PlayerData app
  • Read and write (queries + mutations)
  • Redirect URIs must be pre-approved

Client Credentials Grant — server-to-server.

  • No user sign-in, Client ID + Secret
  • Read-only (queries only)
  • Org-level data access
  • Service account must be granted org access

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
Under the Authorisation Code Grant, data access is governed by club staff membership: to access a club's data, the authenticated user must be listed as staff for that club. If missing, contact the club administrator.

Client Credentials Grant uses organisation-level access — service accounts must be explicitly granted org access by PlayerData.

docs.title: Tokens
docs.text:
• Access tokens expire after 2 hours
  • Authorisation Code Grant: refresh tokens issued — renew via refresh_token grant
  • Client Credentials Grant: no refresh token — re-run flow for new token
  • Refresh proactively before expiry

docs.title: Flows in playerdatapy
docs.text:
Three OAuth2 flows supported via playerdatapy.gqlauth.AuthenticationType:

Flow Use case AUTHORISATION_CODE_FLOW (default) Confidential clients with a secret AUTHORISATION_CODE_FLOW_PCKE Public clients, no secret CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_FLOW Backend-to-backend

Contact support@playerdata.com to request credentials.

docs.title: Client credentials — raw HTTP
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://app.playerdata.co.uk/oauth/token \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials" \
  -d "client_id=$CLIENT_ID" \
  -d "client_secret=$CLIENT_SECRET"

Response:

{
  "access_token": "eyJ...",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 7200
}

Use the access_token as Authorization: Bearer <token> on subsequent requests.

docs.title: Client credentials — Python SDK
docs.text:
from playerdatapy.playerdata_api import PlayerDataAPI
from playerdatapy.gqlauth import AuthenticationType

api = PlayerDataAPI(
    client_id="...",
    client_secret="...",
    authentication_type=AuthenticationType.CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_FLOW,
)

The SDK persists tokens to disk and refreshes proactively. See Python SDK → Authentication.

docs.title: Authorisation Code flow — Python SDK (PKCE)
docs.text:
from playerdatapy.gqlauth import GraphqlAuth, AuthenticationType

auth = GraphqlAuth(
    client_id="...",
    type=AuthenticationType.AUTHORISATION_CODE_FLOW_PCKE,
)

SDK opens a browser, captures the redirect, exchanges code → token, stores it. Override the storage path with token_file=.

docs.title: Authorisation Code flow — manual (any language)
docs.text:
1. Send the user to: 
    https://app.playerdata.co.uk/oauth/authorize
  ?response_type=code
  &client_id=$CLIENT_ID
  &redirect_uri=$REDIRECT_URI
  2. Receive the code parameter on your registered redirect URI.
  3. Exchange for tokens (confidential client — include client_secret): 
    curl -X POST https://app.playerdata.co.uk/oauth/token \
  -d "grant_type=authorization_code" \
  -d "code=$CODE" \
  -d "client_id=$CLIENT_ID" \
  -d "client_secret=$CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -d "redirect_uri=$REDIRECT_URI"
    For public clients, omit client_secret.
  4. Refresh later: 
    curl -X POST https://app.playerdata.co.uk/oauth/token \
  -d "grant_type=refresh_token" \
  -d "refresh_token=$REFRESH_TOKEN" \
  -d "client_id=$CLIENT_ID"

docs.title: Clients
docs.text: The PlayerData API is plain HTTPS + GraphQL. Any tool that can POST JSON works.

docs.title: Python — official SDK
docs.text:
playerdatapy is the recommended Python client. Handles OAuth2, pydantic-typed queries, raw GPS data.

pip install playerdatapy

See Python SDK overview.

docs.title: R — official wrapper
docs.text:
playerdatar is the official R wrapper around the GraphQL API. Useful for analysts working in tidyverse workflows.

# install from GitHub
remotes::install_github("PlayerData/playerdatar")

docs.title: Other languages
docs.text:
Use any GraphQL client or raw HTTP. The pattern is the same:

  1. Obtain an access token via OAuth2 (Authentication)
  2. POST a query to https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphql
  3. Pass the token as Authorization: Bearer <token>

docs.title: JavaScript / TypeScript
docs.text:
graphql-request or urql / Apollo Client:

import { request } from 'graphql-request'

const endpoint = 'https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphql'
const query = `
  query($id: ID!) {
    trainingSession(id: $id) { startTime }
  }
`

const data = await request({
  url: endpoint,
  document: query,
  variables: { id: '77395819-...' },
  requestHeaders: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },
})

docs.title: Ruby / Java / C# / etc.
docs.text: Any GraphQL client library will work. Or use the language's built-in HTTP client and POST the JSON envelope manually.

docs.title: Postman / Insomnia
docs.text: Both speak GraphQL natively. Configure OAuth2 in the auth tab, paste the endpoint, send queries.

docs.title: Codegen from the schema
docs.text:
The schema is available via introspection. Most language ecosystems have a codegen tool that produces typed bindings:

Language Tool Python ariadne-codegen (what playerdatapy uses) TypeScript graphql-codegen Go Khan/genqlient Rust graphql-client Kotlin / Java Apollo Kotlin Swift Apollo iOS

docs.title: Core concepts
docs.text:
Core objects exposed by the API.

Object Description Organisation Top-level entity containing one or more clubs Club Team within an organisation; athletes + staff granted access Person User account, may map to one or more athlete/staff profiles Athlete User profile that participates in sessions; sees only own data Staff User profile that creates, manages, views sessions + all athlete data within a club Session Scheduled activity — Training, Match, or Match with Extra Time SessionParticipation Record of an athlete's involvement in a session Segment Defined time period within a session SegmentParticipation Record of an athlete's involvement in a segment Survey Athlete-reported form data, session-linked or standalone

docs.title: Session subtypes
docs.text:
Two concrete implementations:

  • TrainingSession
  • MatchSession

Distinguish via __typename:

{
  session(id: "...") {
    __typename
    ... on TrainingSession { ... }
    ... on MatchSession { ... }
  }
}

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
PlayerData API uses standard HTTP status codes per the HTTP specification. We do not intentionally return non-standard values.

Reference: IANA HTTP status code registry.

If you encounter an unexpected or non-standard status code, report it to support@playerdata.com — treat as a bug.

docs.title: Common cases
docs.text: Code Meaning 401 Unauthorized Missing or expired access token. Refresh token. 403 Forbidden Authenticated but not staff on the requested club. 400 Bad Request GraphQL validation error — bad query / variables. 429 Too Many Requests Rate or complexity limit hit. Reduce query size. 5xx Server-side fault. Retry with backoff.

docs.title: Pydantic (typed) — recommended
docs.text:
PlayerDataAPI + generated pydantic models. Type-safe field selection, no raw strings.

from playerdatapy.playerdata_api import PlayerDataAPI

api = PlayerDataAPI(client_id="...", client_secret="...")

See examples/pydantic/quick_start.py and the example_use.ipynb notebook for session details, metrics, and raw-data queries.

docs.title: Direct GraphQL
docs.text:
Use gqlclient.Client directly when you want full control of the query string.

See examples/direct/quick_start.py.

docs.title: Sessions in last 30 days
docs.text:
End-to-end example: authenticate, build a time-windowed query, execute, print results.

import asyncio
import os
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from playerdatapy.gqlauth import GraphqlAuth, AuthenticationType
from playerdatapy.gqlclient import Client
from playerdatapy.constants import GRAPHQL_URL

CLIENT_ID = os.environ["CLIENT_ID"]
CLIENT_SECRET = os.environ["CLIENT_SECRET"]
CLUB_ID = os.environ["CLUB_ID"]

auth = GraphqlAuth(
    client_id=CLIENT_ID,
    client_secret=CLIENT_SECRET,
    type=AuthenticationType.CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_FLOW,
)

end = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
start = end - timedelta(days=30)

query = """
query Sessions($clubIdEq: ID!, $startTimeGteq: ISO8601DateTime, $endTimeLteq: ISO8601DateTime) {
  sessions(filter: {
    clubIdEq: $clubIdEq,
    startTimeGteq: $startTimeGteq,
    endTimeLteq: $endTimeLteq
  }) {
    id
    startTime
    sessionParticipations {
      ... on TrainingSessionParticipation {
        athleteMetricSet {
          totalDistanceM
          maxSpeedKph
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
"""

variables = {
    "clubIdEq": CLUB_ID,
    "startTimeGteq": start.isoformat(),
    "endTimeLteq": end.isoformat(),
}


async def main():
    access_token = auth.authenticated_session.token["access_token"]
    client = Client(
        url=GRAPHQL_URL,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}"},
    )
    response = await client.execute(query=query, variables=variables)
    sessions = client.get_data(response)["sessions"]
    for s in sessions:
        print(s["id"], s["startTime"])


asyncio.run(main())

For the typed/pydantic equivalent, see examples/pydantic/quick_start.py.

docs.title: Which authentication method should I use?
docs.text:
• Authorisation Code Grant — user-facing apps, or when mutations are required
  • Client Credentials Grant — backend, read-only integrations

docs.title: Why can't I access a club's data?
docs.text: Using Authorisation Code Grant: the authenticated user/service is not listed as staff for that club. Contact the club administrator to grant access.

docs.title: Can I write or update data (mutate) via the API?
docs.text: Yes, but only under the Authorisation Code Grant.

docs.title: Is the GraphiQL Playground safe to use?
docs.text: Yes — but mutations run against real production data.

docs.title: How long do tokens last?
docs.text: Access tokens expire after 2 hours. Authorisation Code Grant issues refresh tokens; Client Credentials Grant does not (re-run the flow).

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
• Default + maximum page size: 30 records
  • Use offset and limit parameters to page
sessions(filter: {...}, offset: 0, limit: 30) { id }

docs.title: Query complexity
docs.text:
Complexity limits enforced to protect platform stability. Large or deeply nested queries may fail.

Best practice:

  • Split large data requests into smaller queries
  • Avoid requesting full metrics across many sessions in one query
  • Page through sessions, then fetch metrics per page

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
Per-athlete performance metrics on session, segment, and session-participation objects. Every metric on the CommonAthleteMetrics interface is queryable on MatchSessionAthleteMetricSet, TrainingSessionAthleteMetricSet, and their period/segment variants.

Full schema reference: schema/objects/. Live exploration: GraphiQL Playground.

Metrics fall into four shapes:

  1. Totals — one number per athlete per session/segment (e.g. totalDistanceM)
  2. Zonal breakdowns — totals binned into intensity bands (e.g. clubZoneSprintDistanceM)
  3. Time series — sampled values over time at fixed intervals (e.g. distanceMOverTime)
  4. Raw GPS — per-sample latitude / longitude / speed

docs.title: Distance & speed
docs.text:
• totalDistanceM
  • metresPerMinute
  • distanceMAtKph — distance bucketed by speed band
  • timeAtKph — time bucketed by speed band
  • avgSpeedKph
  • maxSpeedKph
  • rawMaxSpeedKph
  • percentageMaxSpeedKph
  • percentageRawMaxSpeedKph
  • ninetyPercentOfMaxSpeedDistanceM
  • ninetyPercentOfMaxSpeedDurationS
  • ninetyPercentOfMaxSpeedEvents

docs.title: 2. Zonal breakdowns
docs.text:
Every zone metric exists in three shapes — *DistanceM, *DurationS, *Events — and in two scopes:

  • Club zones — thresholds shared club-wide
  • Individual zones — per-athlete thresholds

Both scopes are exposed on every athlete. Use whichever matches your analysis.

docs.title: Speed / intensity bands
docs.text:
Ascending order: Jogging → Low intensity → Medium intensity → High intensity → Sprint. HighSpeedRunning is the combined high-intensity + sprint range, not a separate band.

Band Club Individual Jogging clubZoneJogging* individualZoneJogging* Low intensity clubZoneLowIntensity* individualZoneLowIntensity* Medium intensity clubZoneMediumIntensity* individualZoneMediumIntensity* High intensity clubZoneHighIntensity* individualZoneHighIntensity* Sprint clubZoneSprint* individualZoneSprint* High-speed running (combined) clubZoneHighSpeedRunning* individualZoneHighSpeedRunning*

docs.title: Acceleration / deceleration bands
docs.text:
Five-band split (One → Five), each in both scopes.

  • clubZoneOneAcceleration* … clubZoneFiveAcceleration*
  • clubZoneOneDeceleration* … clubZoneFiveDeceleration*
  • individualZoneOneAcceleration* … individualZoneFiveAcceleration*
  • individualZoneOneDeceleration* … individualZoneFiveDeceleration*

docs.title: 4. Raw GPS
docs.text: Latitude, longitude, speed, timestamps per athlete session participation via dedicated schema fields.

docs.title: Configured vs all metrics
docs.text:
Configured metrics — club has enabled in the app. Shown to staff + athletes. Queryable at session, participation, segment level.

All metrics — calculated for every club regardless of config. Useful for analysis or future enablement. Some may not return values depending on firmware.

Caveats:

  • Zonal metrics (speed, acceleration, heart rate) use zones set for the athlete at an individual and club level
  • IMU metrics only available if club device firmware supports them

Best practice:

  • Use configured metrics when aligning with the PlayerData app UI
  • Use all metrics for deeper analysis or custom reporting

docs.title: 3. Run a query
docs.text:
import asyncio
import os
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from playerdatapy.playerdata_api import PlayerDataAPI
from playerdatapy.gqlauth import AuthenticationType

api = PlayerDataAPI(
    client_id=os.environ["CLIENT_ID"],
    client_secret=os.environ["CLIENT_SECRET"],
    authentication_type=AuthenticationType.CLIENT_CREDENTIALS_FLOW,
)

end = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
start = end - timedelta(days=7)

response = asyncio.run(
    api.run_queries(
        "SessionsQuery",
        # Build query objects with helpers from examples/pydantic/queries
        # or directly from playerdatapy.custom_queries / custom_fields.
    )
)
print(response)

See examples/pydantic/quick_start.py for a fully working example using the typed query helpers.

Build/test queries against GraphiQL Playground.

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text: PlayerData exposes a single GraphQL endpoint. Everything — sessions, athletes, metrics, raw GPS — is queryable from one URL.

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text:
https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphql

All requests are POST with a JSON body containing a query (and optional variables).

docs.title: Why GraphQL
docs.text:
• Strongly typed, self-documenting schema
  • Single endpoint, one round-trip for nested data
  • Clients request only the fields they need

Live schema explorer: GraphiQL Playground.

docs.title: Example: raw HTTP
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://app.playerdata.co.uk/api/graphql \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "query($id: ID!) { trainingSession(id: $id) { startTime } }",
    "variables": {"id": "77395819-b377-410a-8d9d-0ed27a771ad7"}
  }'

Response:

{
  "data": {
    "trainingSession": {
      "startTime": "2024-05-19T14:47:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}

  • Concepts — understand the schema's core types

  • Clients — Python SDK + access patterns for other languages

docs.title: Python SDK
docs.text:
playerdatapy — official Python client for the PlayerData GraphQL API.

pip install playerdatapy
# or
uv add playerdatapy

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Typed: pydantic models generated from the schema, autocompletion in your IDE
  • OAuth2 handled: three flows (Authorisation Code, PKCE, Client Credentials), automatic token refresh
  • Async first: built on httpx
  • Raw query escape hatch: Client.execute if you want full control of the query string

docs.title: Get started
docs.text:
• Quickstart — install, authenticate, run your first query
  • Examples — end-to-end runnable scripts
  • API Reference — every public class with signatures, docstrings, inheritance

docs.title: When to use the SDK vs. raw HTTP
docs.text:
Use the SDK Use raw HTTP You're in Python You're in another language You want IDE autocomplete on queries + results You want minimal dependencies You want OAuth2 + refresh handled for you You already have an OAuth2 stack

See Clients for non-Python access patterns.

docs.title: Python SDK — API Reference
docs.text:
Auto-generated reference for every public class in playerdatapy. Pages are rendered from source at build time, so they always match the installed package.

Browse by section in the left navigation. Each class page lists the Related types it uses and the ones that use it.

  • PlayerDataAPI — Recommended entry point. Wraps authentication and runs typed queries/mutations.
  • Authentication — OAuth2 flows and token persistence. Used internally by PlayerDataAPI.
  • GraphQL Client — Low-level async HTTP client. Use for raw GraphQL strings.
  • Queries — Typed query builders generated from the schema. Pass to PlayerDataAPI.run_queries.
  • Mutations — Typed mutation builders. Require Authorisation Code Grant — Client Credentials is read-only.
  • Fields — Field builders used when composing queries and mutations.
  • Input Types — Pydantic models for query/mutation arguments. Field descriptions come from the schema.
  • Enums — String-valued enumerations from the schema. Class and member docstrings come from schema descriptions.
  • Exceptions — Error types raised by the GraphQL client.

docs.title: median_active_athlete_max_acceleration_ms_2 class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
median_active_athlete_max_acceleration_ms_2: ClubGraphQLField = ClubGraphQLField(
    "medianActiveAthleteMaxAccelerationMs2"
)

The median of maximum accelerations (in m/s²) across all active club athletes. Calculates each athlete's top acceleration from their session data, then returns the median. Uses continuous interpolation - if there's an even number of athletes, the result will be the average of the two middle values (e.g., [3.0, 5.0] returns 4.0)

docs.title: median_active_athlete_max_deceleration_ms_2 class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
median_active_athlete_max_deceleration_ms_2: ClubGraphQLField = ClubGraphQLField(
    "medianActiveAthleteMaxDecelerationMs2"
)

The median of maximum decelerations (in m/s²) across all active club athletes. Calculates each athlete's top deceleration from their session data, then returns the median. Uses continuous interpolation - if there's an even number of athletes, the result will be the average of the two middle values (e.g., [3.0, 5.0] returns 4.0)

docs.title: median_active_athlete_top_speed_kph class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
median_active_athlete_top_speed_kph: ClubGraphQLField = (
    ClubGraphQLField("medianActiveAthleteTopSpeedKph")
)

The median of maximum speeds (in kph) across all active club athletes. Calculates each athlete's top speed from their session data, then returns the median. Uses continuous interpolation - if there's an even number of athletes, the result will be the average of the two middle values (e.g., [30, 34] returns 32.0)

Bases: BaseModel

Attributes for assigning relative decelzones to athletes

Show JSON schema: 
{
  "$defs": {
    "DecelzoneLowerBoundsInput": {
      "description": "Deceleration zone boundaries in m/s\u00b2",
      "properties": {
        "zone1": {
          "title": "Zone1",
          "type": "number"
        },
        "zone2": {
          "title": "Zone2",
          "type": "number"
        },
        "zone3": {
          "title": "Zone3",
          "type": "number"
        },
        "zone4": {
          "title": "Zone4",
          "type": "number"
        },
        "zone5": {
          "title": "Zone5",
          "type": "number"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "zone1",
        "zone2",
        "zone3",
        "zone4",
        "zone5"
      ],
      "title": "DecelzoneLowerBoundsInput",
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "description": "Attributes for assigning relative decelzones to athletes",
  "properties": {
    "athleteIds": {
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "title": "Athleteids",
      "type": "array"
    },
    "autoUpdateRelativeDecelzones": {
      "anyOf": [
        {
          "type": "boolean"
        },
        {
          "type": "null"
        }
      ],
      "default": null,
      "title": "Autoupdaterelativedecelzones"
    },
    "customMaxDecelerationMs2": {
      "anyOf": [
        {
          "type": "number"
        },
        {
          "type": "null"
        }
      ],
      "default": null,
      "title": "Custommaxdecelerationms2"
    },
    "relativeDecelzonesLowerBounds": {
      "anyOf": [
        {
          "$ref": "#/$defs/DecelzoneLowerBoundsInput"
        },
        {
          "type": "null"
        }
      ],
      "default": null
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "athleteIds"
  ],
  "title": "AthleteRelativeDecelzoneAttributes",
  "type": "object"
}

Fields:

  • athlete_ids (list[str])
  • auto_update_relative_decelzones (Optional[bool])
  • custom_max_deceleration_ms_2 (Optional[float])
  • relative_decelzones_lower_bounds (Optional[DecelzoneLowerBoundsInput])

docs.title: Sections
docs.text:
• Queries (48) — Read operations exposed on Query
  • Mutations (109) — Write operations exposed on Mutation
  • Object types (296) — Composite types returned by queries
  • Interfaces (11) — Abstract types implemented by objects
  • Unions (6) — Sum types — one of several object types
  • Input types (81) — Argument shapes accepted by operations
  • Enums (51) — Fixed string-valued enumerations
  • Scalars (12) — Primitive types (built-in + custom)

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description allow_font_scaling Enables scaling fonts for accessibility anonymise_data_pipeline_enabled Enables the anonymisation of session data on data recording addition auto_gateway_assignment Enables processing of gateway assignments received from the gateway assignment pipeline auto_pitch_detection Enables auto pitch detection backfill_sampled_speed_blob_job Enables backfilling sampled speed blob job bad_data_clipping Enables the bad data clipping feature in the web application ball_metrics Enables ball metrics browser_report_gen Enable report generation in the browser bt_hr_sensor_pairing Enables setting up an edge to pair with a HR sensor chat Deprecated Enable chat interface chat_extended_capabilities Enable extended chat capabilities including sports science research tools configurable_comparison Enables configurable metric comparisons configurable_metrics Deprecated Enables scoped metric display by club csv_exports Enable survey exports in CSV format custom_csv Enables csv reporting options custom_survey_questions Enables custom survey questions customer_flexible_reporting Enables customer flexible reporting duplicate_segment_null_times Enables segments with nil duration firmware_anchor_updates Enables (self-serve) anchor firmware updates firmware_gateway_updates Enables (self-serve) gateway firmware updates firmware_updates Enables (self-serve) firmware updates, including prompts to update flexible_reporting Enables flexible reporting heartrate_data Deprecated by Configurable Metrics hubspot_integration Enables a variety of integrations with the hubspot API individual_speed_zones Enables the individual speed zones feature localized_terms Enables US / GB specific sports terms (e.g. match -> game) match_session_parts_processing_v2 Enables new match session part processing notification_enhancements Enables the reworked notifications modal pitches_on_sessions Enables reading & writing pitches from Sessions::Session processing_diagnostics Enables processing diagnostics recruitment_dashboard Enables access to the recruitment dashboard via SSO report_improvements Enables the report improvements feature session_automation Enables detected sessions v2 session_quick_actions Enables quick actions on sessions survey_archival Enables archival of surveys sync_only_recent_edges Filters edge sync list to only show edges from a specific session tactical_map_speed Enables speed graphs on tactical maps tactical_video_playback Enables the tactical video playback feature use_competitor_s_metrics Enables using competitor S acceleration/deceleration metrics warmup_match_session Enables warmup match session definitions web_segments Enables segment creation on the web zone_management_v2 Enables the 2026 redesign of individual and club zone management

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description ANCHOR_LOCALISATION_FAILED The anchor auto localisation process failed FILTERED_BY_PITCH_BOUNDS Provided data was (almost) entirely filtered by pitch bounds FILTERED_BY_SATELLITE_COUNT Provided data predominantly below satellite count threshold FILTERED_BY_TIME Provided data was (almost) entirely filtered out by time bounds INSUFFICIENT_LPS_DATA_TO_LOCALISE There was insufficient LPS data to track the athletes position LOW_DATA_POINTS Not enough high speed points to create a pitch from NO_ACCELERATION_DATA There was no acceleration data in the provided file NO_ANCHOR_DATA There was no anchor data recorded NO_DATA_FILE Session reprocessed without datafile NO_DELTA_DATA There was no delta data recorded NO_EDGE_DATA There was no data in the provided file NO_GPS_DATA There was no GPS data in the provided file NO_LPS_DATA There was no LPS data recorded NO_PITCH_DETECTED No pitch was detected for this session NO_UWB_ANCHOR_POSITION_DATA (DEPRECATED) There was no UWB anchor position data in the provided file NO_UWB_DATA (DEPRECATED) There was no UWB data in the provided file NO_UWB_DELTA_TAG_DATA (DEPRECATED) There was no UWB delta tag data in the provided file NO_VALID_LOCALISATION_DATA Could not produce valid LPS tracking data NO_VALID_LPS_DATA There was no valid LPS data recorded PITCH_TOO_LARGE The pitch created by pitch detection was initially too large (150m x 200m) reflecting a high speed in a large area. PITCH_TOO_SMALL The pitch created by pitch detection was initially too small (20m x 50m) reflecting a high speed in a small area. UWB_HYBRID_SYSTEMS_NOT_SUPPORTED (DEPRECATED) Fixed and floating anchors not supported yet UWB_LOCALISATION_FAILED (DEPRECATED) The localisation process failed for the provided UWB data

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description AFTER_SESSION Quick action state after session ends BEFORE_SESSION Quick action state before session starts MATCH_INSIDE_FINAL_SEGMENT Quick action state for match inside final segment MATCH_INSIDE_NON_FINAL_SEGMENT Quick action state for match inside non-final segment MATCH_OUTSIDE_SEGMENT_WITH_FUTURE_SEGMENTS Quick action state for matches to skip past breaks or other gaps between segments TRAINING_INSIDE_SEGMENT_NEAR_SESSION_END Quick action state for training inside segment near session end (10 mins) TRAINING_INSIDE_SEGMENT_NO_FUTURE_SEGMENTS Quick action state for training inside segment with no future segments TRAINING_INSIDE_SEGMENT_WITH_FUTURE_SEGMENTS Quick action state for training inside segment with future segments TRAINING_OUTSIDE_SEGMENT_NEAR_SESSION_END Quick action state for training outside segment near session end (10 mins) TRAINING_OUTSIDE_SEGMENT_NO_FUTURE_SEGMENTS Quick action state for training outside segment with no segments TRAINING_OUTSIDE_SEGMENT_WITH_FUTURE_SEGMENTS Quick action state for training outside segment with future segments UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE Failed to evaluate the correct quick action state for this session

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description ANCHOR_LOCALISATION_FAILED The anchor auto localisation process failed EDGE_SYNC_REQUIRED Session has data syncs but one or more has no data file. FILTERED_BY_PITCH_BOUNDS Provided data was (almost) entirely filtered by pitch bounds FILTERED_BY_SATELLITE_COUNT Provided data predominantly below satellite count threshold FILTERED_BY_TIME Provided data was (almost) entirely filtered out by time bounds FUTURE_SESSION Session start time is in the future. GATEWAY_ASSIGNABLE The session is in progress and a gateway can be assigned. GATEWAY_NOT_CONNECTED The Gateway has not connected to the network. INSUFFICIENT_LPS_DATA_TO_LOCALISE There was insufficient LPS data to track the athletes position LIVE_INDOOR_SESSION Live indoor session's are not supported. LIVE_SESSION_DATA_GAPS The session has gaps in data coverage that require edge sync. LOW_DATA_POINTS Not enough high speed points to create a pitch from NO_ACCELERATION_DATA There was no acceleration data in the provided file NO_ANCHOR_DATA There was no anchor data recorded NO_ATHLETES_ASSIGNED Session has no session participations. NO_DATA_FILE Session reprocessed without datafile NO_DELTA_DATA There was no delta data recorded NO_EDGES_ASSIGNED Session has no data recordings. NO_EDGE_DATA There was no data in the provided file NO_GATEWAY_DATA No data has been received from the Gateway. NO_GPS_DATA There was no GPS data in the provided file NO_LPS_DATA There was no LPS data recorded NO_PITCH_DETECTED No pitch was detected for this session NO_UWB_ANCHOR_POSITION_DATA (DEPRECATED) There was no UWB anchor position data in the provided file NO_UWB_DATA (DEPRECATED) There was no UWB data in the provided file NO_UWB_DELTA_TAG_DATA (DEPRECATED) There was no UWB delta tag data in the provided file NO_VALID_LOCALISATION_DATA Could not produce valid LPS tracking data NO_VALID_LPS_DATA There was no valid LPS data recorded PITCH_TOO_LARGE The pitch created by pitch detection was initially too large (150m x 200m) reflecting a high speed in a large area. PITCH_TOO_SMALL The pitch created by pitch detection was initially too small (20m x 50m) reflecting a high speed in a small area. UWB_HYBRID_SYSTEMS_NOT_SUPPORTED (DEPRECATED) Fixed and floating anchors not supported yet UWB_LOCALISATION_FAILED (DEPRECATED) The localisation process failed for the provided UWB data

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description MatchSession A session that is a match. TrainingSession A session that is a training.

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description EDGE_SYNC_REQUIRED Session has data syncs but one or more has no data file. FUTURE_SESSION Session start time is in the future. GATEWAY_ASSIGNABLE The session is in progress and a gateway can be assigned. GATEWAY_NOT_CONNECTED The Gateway has not connected to the network. LIVE_INDOOR_SESSION Live indoor session's are not supported. LIVE_SESSION_DATA_GAPS The session has gaps in data coverage that require edge sync. NO_ATHLETES_ASSIGNED Session has no session participations. NO_EDGES_ASSIGNED Session has no data recordings. NO_GATEWAY_DATA No data has been received from the Gateway.

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Value Description ALREADY_LINKED This user is already part of this club INVALID_EMAIL The email address is invalid LINK_TO_NEW_CLUB This is an existing user but for a different club NEW_CLAIMABLE_ATHLETE This is a new user with a claimable athlete NEW_USER This is a new user, not already part of a club NO_EMAIL No email address has been provided yet PENDING_MEMBER The application to join the club is waiting for a response from club staff SIGN_IN_REQUIRED To continue, the person must sign in

docs.title: medianActiveAthleteMaxAccelerationMs2
docs.text:
The median of maximum accelerations (in m/s²) across all active club athletes. Calculates each athlete's top acceleration from their session data, then returns the median. Uses continuous interpolation - if there's an even number of athletes, the result will be the average of the two middle values (e.g., [3.0, 5.0] returns 4.0)

Returns: Float

docs.title: medianActiveAthleteMaxDecelerationMs2
docs.text:
The median of maximum decelerations (in m/s²) across all active club athletes. Calculates each athlete's top deceleration from their session data, then returns the median. Uses continuous interpolation - if there's an even number of athletes, the result will be the average of the two middle values (e.g., [3.0, 5.0] returns 4.0)

Returns: Float

docs.title: medianActiveAthleteTopSpeedKph
docs.text:
The median of maximum speeds (in kph) across all active club athletes. Calculates each athlete's top speed from their session data, then returns the median. Uses continuous interpolation - if there's an even number of athletes, the result will be the average of the two middle values (e.g., [30, 34] returns 32.0)

Returns: Float

docs.title: Scalars
docs.text: Name Description BigInt Represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Since the value may exceed the size of a 32-bit integer, it's encoded as a string. Boolean The Boolean scalar type represents true or false. Float The Float scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by IEEE 754. ID The ID scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as "4") or integer (such as 4) input value will be accepted as an ID. ISO8601Date An ISO 8601-encoded date ISO8601DateOrEmptyString An ISO8601 date string with flexibility for empty strings (eg. HTML input value='') ISO8601DateTime An ISO 8601-encoded datetime Int The Int scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1. JSON Represents untyped JSON String The String scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text. TimeValue A tuple where the first element is an iso8601 string and the second element is a float UnsafeISO8601DateTime An ISO 8601-encoded datetime. DateTimes may not be formatted as strictly as 'ISO8601DateTime'

docs.title: BigInt
docs.text: Represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Since the value may exceed the size of a 32-bit integer, it's encoded as a string.

docs.title: Float
docs.text: The Float scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by IEEE 754.

docs.title: ID
docs.text: The ID scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as "4") or integer (such as 4) input value will be accepted as an ID.

docs.title: ISO8601DateOrEmptyString
docs.text: An ISO8601 date string with flexibility for empty strings (eg. HTML input value='')

docs.title: Int
docs.text: The Int scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

docs.title: String
docs.text: The String scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

docs.title: TimeValue
docs.text: A tuple where the first element is an iso8601 string and the second element is a float

docs.title: UnsafeISO8601DateTime
docs.text: An ISO 8601-encoded datetime. DateTimes may not be formatted as strictly as 'ISO8601DateTime'
